title: Spicy Smoky Tender Jamaican Lasagna
description: A pungent, tender, comforting Jamaican dish featuring lasagna sheets, flambe, and a rich, smoky flavor.
instructions: Brown the ground beef in a wok over medium-high heat, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the onion and garlic to the wok and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the jerk seasoning, cumin, smoked paprika, brown sugar, Worcestershire sauce, and scotch bonnet peppers. Cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the tomato paste to the wok and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the lasagna sheets to the wok and cook for 2-3 minutes, until lightly browned.
Transfer the lasagna sheets to a baking dish and top with the ground beef mixture and shredded cheddar cheese.
Flambe the dish with a little bit of rum, if desired, and then place it under the broiler until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the dish from the oven and sprinkle with chopped cilantro and a little bit of flour to thicken the sauce.
Let the dish rest for 10 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
